calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of An Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sydney Hall, via ZOOM, for Theodore Bennett, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

The court confirms that there is an accounting and a declaration in the court's efiling queue, but it appears that they may both be rejected because the documents are incorrectly captioned to refer to the related Trust matter. Mr. Hall confirms that he is not certain if anyone has been appointed as a law practice administrator has been appointed for Ms. Spease's practice and confirms that there is no money in the estate. The court confirms that Clifton Sutton was the sole beneficiary of the will. The court and Mr. Halll discuss the distributions listed in the will. After additional discussion, the court commends Messrs. Hall and Bennett for their work in this matter.

After additional discussion, the court orders the following:

1) The documents pending in the court's efiling queue regarding this matter be filed, regardless of the captions.

2) On its own motion, this Estate is closed for lack of assets.

The matters on today's calendar are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of An Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Feb-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-07-2021 Continued To Feb-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sydney Hall, via ZOOM, for Theodore Bennett, via ZOOM, successor Trustee.

The court requests an update regarding an accounting and Inventory and Appraisal. Mr. Hall reports that his co-counsel had the accounting records in her possession, and that she has since retired and moved out of state, so he does not have access to any records. The court confirms that Clifton Sutton was a beneficiary of the estate. Mr. Bennett reports that Clifton Sutton's Estate remains open and that the only asset in that matter is a home in Texas. After additional discussion, the court confirms that it will be requiring an accounting and Inventory and Appraisal before the court will consider closing the estate.

The Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause are taken off calendar. The Status on Close of Estate, Status of Inventory and Appraisal, and Status of Accounting are continued to Feb-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Show Cause will be set for Dec-07-2021 and issued to Petitioner Theodore Bennett and Attorney Sydney J. Hall to show why they should not be held in contempt and to show why the Executor of the Estate of Clifton R. Sutton should not be surcharged $277,000, of which $10,000 would be payable by the Surety Company. The court reiterates a previous order wherein Mr. Bennett was to provide an accounting from Nov-24-2005 to Oct-30-2021. The Accounting and an Inventory and Appraisal must be filed by Dec-07-2021. Any submissions to the court regarding the Order to Show Cause must be filed by Nov-30-2021 and courtesy copies delivered to the court one week prior to the calling of this matter. The Order to Show Cause is to be served on the Surety Company and all parties served with the previous Order to Appear dated Oct-15-2021.

The Status on Close of Estate and Order to Appear are continued to Dec-07-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Accounting and a Status of Filing of an Inventory and Appraisal is set for Dec-07-2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Continued To Nov-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Charles Andrews, former Petitioner.

The court begins by confirming the absence of attorney Sidney Hall who represents Theodore Bennett, the executor of the deceased personal representative's will. The court then cites probate code 10953 (b) wherein if a personal representative dies or becomes incapacitated and a legal representative is appointed for the deceased or incapacitated personal representative, the legal representative shall file an account of the administration of the deceased or incapacitated personal representative no later than 60 days after appointment. Ms. Ebey advises that she will notify Mr. Hall regarding this matter.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Petitioner Theodore Bennett and Attorney Sidney Hall to appear on Nov-02-2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a Petition for Appointment of Successor Personal Representative or a Petition to Terminate for Lack of Assets, and an Accounting have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Amanda Ebey, appearing via ZOOM, counsel for Charles Andrews, Petitioner.

Court requests status of the petition. Counsel advises that the Petitioner will not be moving forward with the petition and requests that the matter be taken off calendar.

The Court grants the request to take the petition off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: S. Le/R. Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Sidney Hall for Contestant.

The Court stated that it is still waiting for a response to the Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Hall stated that he e-filed his objections yesterday. Ms. Wenzel stated that her Client wishes to withdraw his petition. The Petition for Letters of Administration is withdrawn and off calendar.
